items.headline: Los Angeles Kings Colorado Avalanche Game 1 recap April 19, 2026
items.description: DENVER -- Scott Wedgewood made 24 saves in his first career Stanley Cup Playoff start, and the Colorado Avalanche defeated the Los Angeles Kings 2-1 in Game 1 of the Western Conference First Round at Ball Arena on Sunday.

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres playoff arrivals vintage cars
items.description:
Talk about arriving in style.
Ahead of Game 1 of the Eastern Conference First Round between the Buffalo Sabres and Boston Bruins at KeyBank Center on Sunday, Sabres players arrived to the arena in stylish vintage cars that are sure to bring up some feelings of nostalgia.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description:
The 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs are underway, with three games played Saturday and four more to be played Sunday.
Here is the complete schedule for the first round of the playoffs, including TV information and results:

items.headline: Jamie Drysdale Trevor Zegras savor ful circle moment with Philadelphia Flyers
items.description:
PITTSBURGH -- Jamie Drysdale called it a "full-circle moment."
Drysdale scored the Philadelphia Flyers' first goal of the Stanley Cup Playoffs in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Pittsburgh Penguins on Saturday, assisted by Trevor Zegras. Philadelphia won 3-2.
It was playoff success each expected to

items.headline: Leon Draisaitl could play Game 1 for Edmonton against Anaheim
items.description:
EDMONTON -- Leon Draisaitl looks ready to go for the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.
The way the Edmonton Oilers forward was skating in practice Sunday seemed to indicate he’ll be in the lineup for Game 1 of the Western Conference First Round against the Anaheim Ducks at Rogers Place on

items.headline: Sidney Crosby history against Philadelphia Flyers could spark Pittsburgh Penguins
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- Sidney Crosby has a complicated relationship with Pennsylvania.
In Pittsburgh, nestled in the foothills of the southwestern portion of the commonwealth, the 38-year-old center is a hero. He has won the Stanley Cup three times (2009, 2016, 2017) with its Pittsburgh Penguins.

items.headline: Frederik Andersen starts Eastern First Round strong for Carolina looks to do it again Game 2
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. -- Frederik Andersen sat in the shadows for the Carolina Hurricanes for so long this season.
A focal point of this team since his arrival as a big-ticket free agent in 2021, the goalie was suddenly pushed into the background while Brandon Bussi Mania ran wild.

items.headline: Dallas Stars enter Game 2 against Minnesota Wild familiar with handling early adversity
items.description:
DALLAS -- The Dallas Stars aren't confused or wondering why they're trailing the Minnesota Wild in the Western Conference First Round entering Game 2 on Monday.
The score of Game 1 on Saturday was a perfect indication of how the game went.

items.headline: Rasmus Ristolainen poised play helps Philadelphia Flyers to Game 1 win
items.description:
PITTSBURGH -- Rasmus Ristolainen heard for 13 seasons that his game was built for the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
At 6-foot-4, 208 pounds, with nastiness and physicality hard-wired into his game, he certainly looked like someone who could be a postseason difference-maker.
On Saturday, he finally got to show it.
